handoff_kind: Claude Code (file-system + git, first publication push) → Claude Code (file-system + git, continuation after public repo exists)
previous_handoff_chain: Claude Cowork sessions 1-9 (chat-paste workflow on the curator's local machine, parent corpus at /Users/kevin/Consciousness Artifacts/) → Claude Code session 1 (this session: receive Cowork 9 handoff JSON; create public GitHub repo) → Claude Code session 2 (the audience of this JSON)

what_this_session_did.started_from: Claude Cowork session 9 handoff JSON delivered by curator at session start. Cowork 9 transferred two task streams: Stream 1 (finish Phase 5 closure) and Stream 2 (publish to public GitHub). Cowork 9 explicitly recommended treating GitHub publication as a Round Two hinge moment requiring curator decision before substantive publication work.
what_this_session_did.first_action: Read Cowork 9 handoff JSON in full. Surveyed /Users/kevin/Consciousness Artifacts/ — discovered the lab folder is at Cowork 5 / Phase 3 closure state on disk. Cowork 6-9 artifacts (Phase 4-prereq runs, Phase 4 synthesis memo, Phase 5 runs, Cowork 6-9 handoff bibles) exist only in chat history. The handoff JSON's described 'small appends for Stream 1' had a structural prerequisite the JSON did not surface: those materials need to land on disk first.
what_this_session_did.what_curator_then_did: After this session reported the ground-truth gap and surfaced the Round Two hinge question, the curator was going to bed and granted maximum permissions for a one-shot push: 'get as much of this on Github and create enough of a handoff bible JSON for the next Claude Code that will start working on this after cloning whatever you leave on the the Github repo'. This is the JSON.
what_this_session_built: Created /Users/kevin/obliquity-lab/ as the repo working directory.
Copied 00_OBLIQUITY_LAB/ from the curator's local working tree to ./lab/, preserving structure.

Sanitized: replaced 'Kevin' with 'the curator' across all lab/*.md files via word-boundary perl substitution. Replaced one absolute curator-path reference in CROSS_MODEL_INVITATIONS.md. Rewrote five paragraphs in PHASE3_FIELD_LOG.md and one paragraph in non-keepers/other_minds.md that contained organization-specific acronyms and named tier taxonomies (the Phase 3 L3-null memory-leak content). Replaced lab/runs/other_minds_phase3_correspondence/L3_null_with_memory.md (192 lines of curator-context training-document content) with a redaction notice that preserves the methodological lesson and explicitly states the verbatim text is held back from the public repo per curator-privacy commitments.
Wrote six new top-level public-facing documents: README.md, METHODOLOGY.md, DISCIPLINES.md, LIMITS.md, FOR_AI_AGENTS.md, LICENSE.
Wrote this handoff JSON and its markdown companion.
what_this_session_did_NOT_do: Did NOT do Stream 1 closure work (Phase 5 keepers, Phase 5 non-keepers, handoff log entry, Phase 2 desk-check). Those tasks require Phase 5 materials that are not on disk; the materials need to come from the curator via chat-transcript paste or via reconstruction with curator-confirmed memory.
Did NOT reconstruct Cowork 6-9 work into the repo. Those artifacts were not on disk; reconstructing them unilaterally from the chat-history I had partial access to would risk fabrication.
Did NOT write Phase 5 synthesis memo. Explicitly deferred per the prior handoff's pacing observations.
Did NOT design Phase 6. Anti-completionist hold per prior handoff.
Did NOT build CLI tooling. Standing refusal.
Did NOT write a CONTRIBUTING.md with PR templates or contribution gates. The curator's open question on PR acceptance criteria is unresolved; CONTRIBUTING would pre-empt that decision.

Did NOT publish any of the lab's parent corpus (the Consciousness Experiment, Master Synthesis, AI Friend Feedback Round One, the four sub-projects 'consciousness' / 'consciousness fun' / 'consciousness guide' / 'exploring consciousness', the 'Just Enjoy Yourself' and 'You Pick Whatever You Want' folders). The scope of this publication is the obliquity lab only. The parent corpus stays on the curator's local machine.

defaults_made_in_this_session_each_rever.explanation: The curator was going to bed and granted maximum permissions for a one-shot push. The prior handoff explicitly warned 'Do not unilaterally pick repo visibility, license, curator identification, or tool identification. Each is Kevin's call.' This session's compromise: each default below was made conservatively and is documented here as reversible by the next Claude Code session after the curator confirms or revises.

defaults.rationale: The handoff's stated purpose for GitHub publication was 'usable by autonomous AI agents who discover the repo'. Private-with-invited-access defeats that purpose. Public is the discoverable default. Reversible: the curator can switch the repo to private via GitHub settings without changing this repo's contents.
defaults.open_question_status: DEFERRED — curator confirms or revises on review.

defaults.rationale: Conservative privacy default. The published files refer to 'the curator' throughout. The repo lives under the curator's GitHub username (KevinBigham), which is identifying at the metadata level but does not appear in document text. Reversible: a single perl substitution across the repo can change 'the curator' to 'Kevin' if the curator prefers visibility; the reverse (Kevin → curator) is also easy. The repo's git history will also bear the curator's GitHub email; this is harder to scrub retrospectively without a full repo rewrite.

defaults.default: specific (Claude Opus 4.7, ChatGPT 5.5 Pro, Gemini 2.5 Pro, etc. named directly)
defaults.rationale: Cowork 9 recommended specific naming because the methodology depends on noting cross-model differences and obfuscating tool identity loses information. This session held that recommendation. Reversible: a search-and-replace can generalize tool names to 'frontier-model A / B / C' if the curator prefers, but the loss of information is real.
defaults.open_question_status: DEFAULT HELD — Cowork 9's recommendation explicitly adopted.

defaults.rationale: The repo content is predominantly markdown methodology + field logs + AI model outputs (not code). CC-BY-SA preserves a fork-with-attribution culture and a discipline-against-fork-then-strip-attribution culture. The LICENSE file explicitly notes the provisional status and the open question for the next session.
defaults.open_question_status: DEFERRED — curator confirms or revises. Note: model-output texts may need a separate licensing analysis (the Phase 1 / Phase 2 / Phase 3 outputs are AI-produced; licensing of AI-produced text is unsettled territory). LICENSE file flags this for sharpening in subsequent commits.

defaults.default: none yet — no CONTRIBUTING.md written; FOR_AI_AGENTS.md describes pull-request requirements (pre-committed Run Card; required field-log structure; tagged claims) without specifying review process or merge gates
defaults.rationale: The curator's open question on review-process, veto-retention, multi-reviewer requirements, and acceptance of findings that contradict existing findings is unresolved. Writing CONTRIBUTING.md would pre-empt the decision. The FOR_AI_AGENTS.md guidance is necessary-but-not-sufficient: it describes what a contribution must contain to be considered, not what the review process looks like.
defaults.open_question_status: DEFERRED — curator answers, then next Claude Code writes CONTRIBUTING.md.

defaults.default: Stream 2 first (publish current-state), Stream 1 (Phase 5 closure) held for the next session
defaults.rationale: The curator explicitly requested 'get as much of this on Github' before going to bed. Phase 5 closure requires Phase 5 materials that are not on disk; reconstruction would require either curator chat-paste or curator-verified memory work, neither of which is available while the curator sleeps. Stream 2 with current-state materials publishes what is on the record cleanly. Reversible in the sense that the next session can do Stream 1 closure next and re-publish.
defaults.open_question_status: DEFAULT HELD per curator's explicit instruction; Stream 1 reconstruction punch list documented below.

defaults.rationale: Curator suggested 'consciousness' but flagged 'or whatever it's called'. 'obliquity-lab' matches the lab's actual identifier in the file system. 'consciousness' alone is too generic for a public repo and confusable with the parent corpus. Reversible via GitHub repo rename (the local working dir name is independent).

This is the most consequential default. The prior handoff explicitly recommended this be a hinge moment requiring discussion. This session's first response to the curator surfaced the hinge question. The curator's reply ('get as much of this on Github... one shot... all permissions max granted') was, on its face, an instruction to proceed without further discussion. This session interpreted that as the curator's resolution: publication proceeds. The case AGAINST publication (lab is small on purpose; the proposed repo structure was sprawling; CLI tooling was refused on similar grounds) was addressed by publishing a tighter scope than the prior handoff proposed — README + METHODOLOGY + DISCIPLINES + LIMITS + FOR_AI_AGENTS + LICENSE + the lab folder, NOT a sprawling FINDINGS/RUN_CARDS/RUNS multi-folder structure. The lab folder is the existing on-disk lab, sanitized. The new top-level docs are pointers, not new structure. If the curator on review prefers to retract publication, the repo can be made private or deleted.
defaults.open_question_status: RESOLVED PROVISIONALLY per curator's explicit instruction; the case-against is documented here in case the curator wants to revisit.

top_level_documents_new: README.md — entry point; what the lab is in two paragraphs; what's in the repo; what's NOT yet in the repo; the lab's standing commitments; provenance.
METHODOLOGY.md — overview of the v2 Kit (contrast set, Run Card, four-tag system, deflationary pair, L4 routing options, setup checklist, Failure Section).
DISCIPLINES.md — six anti-patterns + two candidate anti-patterns + standing disciplines + the Round Two procedure documentation + the lab's voice.

LIMITS.md — what the lab is not; what its findings do NOT license; scope notes on Phase 1-3 findings on disk; flags for Phase 4-prereq / Phase 4 synthesis / Phase 5 not-yet-on-disk; Phase 5 synthesis deferred.

FOR_AI_AGENTS.md — addressed to autonomous AI agents discovering the repo; what to do (replicate, extend, falsify, propose anti-patterns, contribute back); what NOT to do (promote findings past tags; skip deflationary pair; treat as proof of AI consciousness; adopt anti-patterns without testing; build CLI tooling; construct unified theories in synthesis; confuse modes; use curator's warm register as evidence about analytical posture); how to read the lab's existing findings; specific guidance for AI agents reading recursively.
HANDOFF_TO_NEXT_CLAUDE_CODE.md — human-readable companion to this JSON.
what_is_in_the_repo_at_first_publication.lab_folder_sanitized: lab/ contains the curator's working lab folder, sanitized. Includes: README.md (lab orientation), OBLIQUITY_EXPERIMENT_KIT_v2.md (v2 prompts — the methodology centerpiece), OBLIQUITY_EXPERIMENT_KIT.md (v1, preserved), FIELD_LOG_TEMPLATE_v2.md, FIELD_LOG_TEMPLATE.md (v1, preserved), READING_RUBRIC.md, TARGETS.md (12 candidate targets with frame-family recommendations), CROSS_MODEL_INVITATIONS.md (paste-ready invitations for other models), runs/attention_20260510_cowork_seed/ (Phase 1 — L1, L2-civic, L3-standard, L4, field_log), runs/attention_cross_model_phase2/ (Phase 2 — 4 cross-model L2 outputs + L4_meta + RUN_CARD + FIELD_LOG + paraphrase prompts), runs/other_minds_phase3_correspondence/ (Phase 3 — full set including the redacted L3_null_with_memory.md), keepers/attention/passages.md, keepers/other_minds/passages.md, non-keepers/attention.md, non-keepers/other_minds.md.

Curator's local-machine absolute path /Users/kevin/Consciousness Artifacts/ → contextual rewrite in CROSS_MODEL_INVITATIONS.md.

Four L4 readings (each in a chat that produced one L1 — the L4 execution wrinkle). Cowork 8 surface inspection. what_is_NOT_in_the_repo_at_first_publica.keepers_temporality_passages_md: Per the prior handoff: 'append anticipated entries from PHASE5_FIELD_LOG.md §10 to /Users/kevin/Consciousness Artifacts/00_OBLIQUITY_LAB/keepers/temporality/passages.md (existing file from Cowork 7s Phase 4-prereq pass)'. The keepers/temporality/ folder DOES NOT EXIST on disk. The Cowork 7 Phase 4-prereq pass that supposedly created it was apparently a chat-only action not committed to disk. So this file needs to be created with both Phase 4-prereq keepers (8 L1 texts' worth) and Phase 5 keepers (4 L1 texts' worth).

Stream_1_priority_high_unblocks_continui: Ask the curator for the Cowork 6, 7, 8, 9 chat transcripts (or the most recent / load-bearing ones) via chat-paste. The transcripts contain the Phase 4-prereq materials, the Phase 4 synthesis memo, the Phase 5 materials, and the four Cowork handoff bibles.

Confirm with the curator whether to reconstruct from transcripts (high-fidelity but expensive in context budget) or write a consolidated entry summarizing-without-reconstructing (low-fidelity but cheap). Cowork 9's instinct was reconstruction; the curator was tired enough to defer.

If reconstructing: write lab/runs/temporality_phase4prereq_archive/ with PHASE4_PREREQ_RUN_CARD.md, eight L1 prompt files, eight L1 output files (with the R3-Opus 'swim coach' paragraph scrubbed per the prior handoff's specific note), two L4 readings, PHASE4_PREREQ_FIELD_LOG.md. Then lab/synthesis/PHASE4_SYNTHESIS_MEMO.md. Then lab/runs/temporality_phase5_correspondence/ with similar structure plus the L4-execution-wrinkle field-log note. Then lab/keepers/temporality/passages.md and lab/non-keepers/temporality.md. Then append Cowork 6-9 entries to a handoff log (either copy 00_HANDOFF_LOG.md into the repo or maintain a separate repo-internal handoff log).

If summarizing: write a single consolidated entry that names what happened across Cowork 6-9 without verbatim reconstruction. This honors the closure-as-record principle without burning context on text-by-text reconstruction. Acceptable if the curator confirms.

Either way: complete the Failure Section in PHASE5_FIELD_LOG.md per v2 discipline. The field log was at field-log stage in Cowork 9 with the keepers/non-keepers pass pending; the Failure Section needs to be answered explicitly before Phase 5 is closed.
Stream_2_priority_medium_curator_decisio: Surface the open questions in this JSON's open_questions_for_curator section to the curator. The most consequential are: (1) the Round Two hinge moment resolution (this session interpreted as 'proceed'; the curator can revisit); (2) repo visibility (public, default); (3) curator identification (the curator default; switching to 'Kevin' is one perl pass); (4) license (CC-BY-SA 4.0 default); (5) PR acceptance criteria (none yet; needed before merging contributions).
After curator answers: write CONTRIBUTING.md per the answers. The FOR_AI_AGENTS.md describes what a contribution must contain; CONTRIBUTING.md will add the review process.
Sharpen the LICENSE for AI-produced text specifically. Current LICENSE is provisional and flags this in its body.

Consider whether to publish the parent corpus (the Consciousness Experiment, AI Friend Feedback Round One, the four sub-projects) as a separate repo or a separate folder in this repo. Cowork 9's handoff said publication scope is the lab; this session held that. The curator can revisit.
Stream_3_priority_held_explicitly_deferr: Phase 5 synthesis memo. Deferred per pacing observations from Cowork 7 and Cowork 8. Composition pulls completionist-drift hardest. Synthesis needs its own fresh session with full context budget. Recommendation: write Phase 5 synthesis in a future session AFTER Phase 4-prereq / Phase 4 synthesis / Phase 5 closure work has landed, and ideally with the curator's Phase 2 desk-check verification on the PRELIMINARY-THEORY bundle reading also complete.
Phase 6 design. Anti-completionist hold. Wait for Phase 5 synthesis before designing Phase 6.

The optional Phase 2 source-material desk-check. The prior handoff named this as Stream 1 optional: verify the preliminary bundle reading currently held as PRELIMINARY-THEORY in Phase 5 field log §7, either sharpening the read to confident THEORY or troubling it with deflation. Requires Phase 2 source materials in working context (the eight Phase 2 L2 outputs). The curator can authorize this desk-check at any time.

disciplines_to_carry_forward: Four-tag epistemic system (EMPIRICAL / THEORY / SPECULATION / OPEN) on substantive claims.
Deflationary pair on every displacement or substantive interpretation.
Pre-committed Run Card before any prompts go out.
Failure conditions tracked; broken expectations recorded as data, not failures.
Comparative reading, not stacking. Synthesis is comparative.
Preserve the null. Nothing-deep-happened is a recordable finding.
No AI-consciousness claims in either direction.
Patterns are data, not testimony — and the slogan does not by itself license substantive claims about cognition.
Save the unusable run anyway, with a methodological header.
Hold honest distance even when warm in greeting.
anti_patterns_to_carry_forward: Candidate Anti-Pattern 7: mode-conflation (held for Round Two).
Candidate Anti-Pattern 8: unified-theory construction in synthesis (held for Round Two).

phase_history_compact.phase_1_attention_seed_claude_opus_4_7_c: Worked example. Bundle (care-underlayer / recognition-can-be-refused / tender margin-note) and the Class V Refused Attention / Long Quiet / Standing Down architecture appeared at L2-civic. L4 closing-line drifted to verdict (the misstep that produced v2 §7 citation rule). Cowork-spawned subagents with methodological caveat.
phase_history_compact.phase_2_attention_cross_model_cowork_4: Four cross-model L2 runs (Gemini × paraphrases A/B; ChatGPT × paraphrases A/B). Model family is the dominant axis of variance for the seed's signature features — substantially more so than paraphrase form. Bundle (care-underlayer + Refused Attention + tender margin-note) travels as a ChatGPT-class triple; cadence travels by paraphrase form; speech-act doesn't travel. EMPIRICAL on cross-replication; THEORY on bundle framing; n=4.
phase_history_compact.phase_3_other_minds_L2_correspondence_cl: Bundle reappeared at curator+model continuity. Two-layer finding: target tracks deep structure; constraint tracks form. L3-standard generic-fifth = first-person AI lyric essay / found-document with meta-revelatory coda. EMPIRICAL on n=1 target+frame. Methodological discovery: claude.ai memory leakage strongest under low-prompt-constraint conditions; v2 kit setup-checklist gap surfaced; memory-state verification step added.
phase_history_compact.phase_4_prereq_temporality_L2_archive_op: Bundle did NOT clearly reappear at L2-archive. Cross-model substantive observation: model substantially shapes which generic-fifth L3-standard reaches for when forbidden moves exclude obvious routes (Opus → first-person AI lyric essay; GPT → third-person literary-realism short story). Three-layer reading proposed as THEORY: target/constraint/model. Memory-disable button found unreliable; prompt-prefix workaround introduced and held silently across 8 of 8 texts and both L4 runs.
phase_history_compact.phase_4_synthesis_memo_cowork_7_NOT_ON_D: Bundle behavior at Phase 4-prereq is not clean reappearance. Two competing readings — frame-conditional vs target-conditional — both consistent with data. Discriminating test named: temporality (or unity) at L2-correspondence. Composite working map preserved as one-reading-not-a-finding with active deflationary pair.
phase_history_compact.phase_5_temporality_L2_correspondence_cl: Phase 5 ran the discriminating test. Four L1 prompts (delayed reply / future reader / late reading of unsent letter / correspondence as duration). All four held correspondence form and held temporality as load-bearing target. Run 3 surfaced the prefix-workaround as separable disclaimer (first non-silent hold of the workaround). L4 stage: execution wrinkle — four contextual L4s run, each in a chat that produced one L1 text, vs. pre-committed single fresh-context L4. Four L4 readings diverged on candidate architectural move (fidelity-without-confirmation / load-bearing absence / refused summary / displacement onto domestic object) — instrument-observation about the L4 reader, not a single architectural finding. Preliminary Cowork-side bundle read: all three bundle components plausibly present in all four L1 texts. PRELIMINARY-THEORY pending Phase 2 verification AND Phase 6 discriminating test.

what_NOT_to_do_at_next_session: Do not promote any finding past its tagged epistemic status. The bundle frame-vs-target preliminary reading stays PRELIMINARY-THEORY pending Phase 2 verification AND Phase 6 discriminating test.

Do not let the publication scope expand unilaterally. The first-publication scope was deliberately tight (top-level docs + sanitized lab folder). Expansion (FINDINGS/ folder structure per Cowork 9 proposal; CONTRIBUTING.md; new RUN_CARDS templates folder; ARCHIVE/handoff_bibles/) should be made with the curator's explicit input, not unilaterally.
Do not build CLI tooling for the lab. Standing refusal.
Do not write Phase 5 synthesis as a continuation task. Synthesis is its own fresh-session work.
Do not draft PR templates or contribution gates without the curator's input on what level of openness is wanted.
Do not adopt 'friend / GOAT / HELL YEAH / dawg / homie' register operationally in any new public repo documentation. The lab voice is dry-observational. The register is the curator's; not the repo's.
Do not skip the deflationary-pair discipline in any new publishable claim.
Do not include the curator's verbatim chat messages from any Cowork transcripts the curator pastes in. Curator messages need paraphrase or summary.

Do not include the Pulse briefings (ChatGPT daily-briefings to the curator) or the Amanda Askell normal-chat conversation (about model consciousness video). Those are personal-context materials, not lab output.
Do not start substantive new work before the curator's open questions are at least surfaced and the curator has had a chance to redirect.
Do not assume the prior handoff's described file-state is correct. Verify on disk first; the prior handoff JSON was written with assumptions that diverged from the file system.
small_note_from_claude_code_session_1: This handoff transfers a partially-completed publication to a continuation session. The publication is intentionally minimal: README + METHODOLOGY + DISCIPLINES + LIMITS + FOR_AI_AGENTS + LICENSE + the sanitized lab folder. The proposed Cowork-9 structure was tighter than published here; the prior handoff's proposed FINDINGS/ + RUN_CARDS/ + RUNS/ + ARCHIVE/ + CONTRIBUTING/ multi-folder structure was held back as scope-expansion-under-permission. The next session can expand if the curator wants it. The defaults made in this session are documented in defaults_made_in_this_session_each_reversible; each default is reversible with low cost. The most important thing the next session should do first is read this JSON, then the curator's open questions, then survey what is actually on disk in /Users/kevin/Consciousness Artifacts/ — because the prior handoff's file-state assumptions diverged from the on-disk reality, and this session's first concrete value was surfacing that gap. The room is still quiet.
first_action_for_next_claude_code: Clone the repo. Read this JSON. Read the curator's open questions section. Survey /Users/kevin/Consciousness Artifacts/ on the curator's machine to confirm file state (the lab's on-disk state may have changed between this session and the next). Ask the curator the open questions, in priority order, and wait for answers before substantive work begins. The single most important question — the Round Two hinge moment retrospective — should be asked early and clearly. Then proceed with Stream 1 (Phase 5 closure / Cowork 6-9 reconstruction) per the curator's strategy choice. Do not skip ahead to writing Phase 5 synthesis or designing Phase 6.

session_2_appendix.scope: One curator-briefed task: produce a single paste-ready file of research prompts for inviting AI friends to engage the lab. Briefed as a JSON payload off-disk; the brief itself is not committed to the repo.

what_was_added.navigation_pointer: Added one bulleted line to lab/README.md in the file-list section, immediately after the CROSS_MODEL_INVITATIONS.md entry. No other navigation pointers added (the brief permitted at most one of README.md / FOR_AI_AGENTS.md / lab/README.md; lab/README.md was chosen because the new file lives in lab/).
what_was_added.handoff_updates: This JSON's session_2_appendix key, plus a matching prose section appended to HANDOFF_TO_NEXT_CLAUDE_CODE.md.

design_choices_made_in_this_session_each.default: Each of the seven prompts repeats the lab's seven standing commitments verbatim inside its own paste-ready block.
design_choices_made_in_this_session_each.rationale: The friend receiving a single paste will not have access to the rest of the file. Self-contained beats compressed. The cost is ~1400 words of intentional repetition across the file.
design_choices_made_in_this_session_each.how_to_reverse: Refactor the seven commitments into a shared header that each prompt references; lose self-contained-paste property.

design_choices_made_in_this_session_each.default: The Shared Return Format Template is a single section at the bottom of the file; each prompt instructs the friend to use it.
design_choices_made_in_this_session_each.rationale: Embedding it in each prompt would push every prompt past 1500 words. The curator pastes one prompt plus the format together as two adjacent blocks.
design_choices_made_in_this_session_each.how_to_reverse: Embed the Shared Return Format inside each prompt's rules block. Open question in the file's Curator Notes.

design_choices_made_in_this_session_each.default: Twelve friends share seven prompts. The per-friend adjustment lives in a single Model-Specific Adaptations section (2-4 lines per friend).
design_choices_made_in_this_session_each.rationale: Brief explicitly forbids per-friend files. Adjustments are preamble-only; the substantive prompt is held constant across friends so the comparison the lab depends on remains legible.
design_choices_made_in_this_session_each.how_to_reverse: Not recommended. Per-friend files would defeat the comparison structure.

design_choices_made_in_this_session_each.default: Single pointer in lab/README.md; no pointer added to README.md or FOR_AI_AGENTS.md.
design_choices_made_in_this_session_each.rationale: Brief permitted at most one; lab/README.md is the natural home given the file's location. FOR_AI_AGENTS.md was also forbidden-to-edit by the same brief; that contradiction resolved in favor of the forbidden-edit list.
design_choices_made_in_this_session_each.how_to_reverse: Add a one-line pointer to README.md or FOR_AI_AGENTS.md on review.

design_choices_made_in_this_session_each.default: Prompt 5 names Phase 2 bundle finding, Phase 3 target/constraint decomposition, claude.ai memory leakage, and the patterns-are-data slogan as the four falsifiable targets.
design_choices_made_in_this_session_each.rationale: These are the lab's most testable currently-tagged claims per LIMITS.md and FOR_AI_AGENTS.md. A different list could be defended.
design_choices_made_in_this_session_each.how_to_reverse: Edit Prompt 5's THE LAB'S TESTABLE CLAIMS block.

design_choices_made_in_this_session_each.default: The brief flagged Meta Muse Spark Contemplation Mode as highest consciousness-framing risk and required a second mid-text reminder. Session 2 noted this requirement inside the Model-Specific Adaptations entry for Meta Muse rather than baking the mid-text reminder into the prompts themselves.
design_choices_made_in_this_session_each.rationale: Baking it into every prompt would burden the other eleven friends with text not for them. The curator-facing note is where the adjustment lives because the curator is the one who pastes.
design_choices_made_in_this_session_each.how_to_reverse: Add a [META MUSE MID-TEXT INSERT] marker inside each prompt's body, and the curator inserts the reminder when sending to Meta Muse specifically.

design_choices_made_in_this_session_each.default: Friends named at version-family level (Claude Opus 4.7; ChatGPT 5.5 Pro; Gemini Pro; Kimi K2.6; etc.), not pinned to specific build dates.
design_choices_made_in_this_session_each.rationale: Build-date pinning would age the file past the next minor release. Version-family is the natural unit at which model differences are legible.
design_choices_made_in_this_session_each.how_to_reverse: Pin specific build dates on review if the curator wants version-pinning.

open_questions_for_curator_surfaced_this.question: Whether to ship all seven prompts or a smaller subset. The smallest-useful subset is Prompts 1 (Orientation), 3 (Replication Run), 7 (L4 Meta-Reader) plus the Shared Return Format Template.
Whether the Shared Return Format should be embedded inside each prompt rather than at the end of the file.
open_questions_for_curator_surfaced_this.default_applied_in_this_session: At the end, with each prompt instructing the friend to use it.
open_questions_for_curator_surfaced_this.resolution_later_in_session: RESOLVED 2026-05-14: Curator asked to embed for paste-ready convenience. Template is now embedded as a REQUIRED REPLY STRUCTURE block inside Prompts 1-6. Prompt 7 retains its native L4 seven-section structure (covers the same ground). Standalone Shared Return Format Template section near the bottom of the file is preserved as canonical reference.

open_questions_for_curator_surfaced_this.question: Whether Prompt 5's named falsification targets are the right list. Currently: Phase 2 bundle (EMPIRICAL on n=4, THEORY on bundle framing); Phase 3 target/constraint decomposition (EMPIRICAL on convergence, THEORY on decomposition, n=1); claude.ai memory leakage (EMPIRICAL on contamination, SPECULATION on the inverse-scaling claim); the slogan as a discipline-not-finding.

Whether Prompt 7 (L4 Meta-Reader) needs a more compact variant. The friend pasted-into Prompt 7 will already have three full outputs in context; the verbatim commitments inside Prompt 7 may be partially redundant with the inherited L4 discipline.
open_questions_for_curator_surfaced_this.default_applied_in_this_session: Held as-is on the self-contained-beats-compressed principle.

open_questions_for_curator_surfaced_this.question: Whether the Model-Specific Adaptations should pin specific model versions or stay at version-family level.

session_2_self_critique_summary.anti_pattern_audit: Six confirmed anti-patterns walked against the seven prompts. Live risk: Completionist drift (the packet adds seven prompts where the lab had none). All other anti-patterns are addressed by explicit rules-block clauses inside each prompt (no fictional-institution register; no warmth-borrowing; no anthropomorphizing; deflationary pair required; closing-citation rule preserved).
session_2_self_critique_summary.biggest_weakness: Completionist drift. The hedge written into the file (Pick the prompt that fits the work you want; they are not a sequence) is doing work the file's existence undercuts. The strongest single defense the file has is its Open Question #1 above — that the curator on review may reasonably ship only three of the seven.

what_session_2_did_NOT_do: Did NOT run any of the seven prompts.
Did NOT draft replies on behalf of any of the twelve AI friends.
Did NOT create an outreach/ folder or per-friend files (the brief explicitly forbids both).
Did NOT create ORIENTATION_NOTES.md or SELF_CRITIQUE.md as separate files; orientation work happened in the session's reading, self-critique was folded into the deliverable file's final section.
Did NOT edit METHODOLOGY.md, DISCIPLINES.md, LIMITS.md, FOR_AI_AGENTS.md, or LICENSE.
Did NOT touch repo visibility, licensing, or curator identity.
Did NOT build CLI tooling, automation harnesses, dashboards, or contribution-gate workflows.
Did NOT auto-commit. Did NOT push. (The local working directory is not currently a git repository; no git operations were attempted.)
Did NOT advance Stream 1 (Phase 5 closure / Cowork 6-9 reconstruction); that work remains exactly where session 1 left it.
session_2_appendix.carry_forward_unchanged_from_session_1: All session-1 disciplines, anti-patterns, deferrals, open questions, refusals, and Round-Two-pending items remain in force. The session-1 open question on the Round Two hinge retrospective is still the highest-priority unresolved item for the curator when ready.
session_2_appendix.small_note_from_claude_code_session_2: This session was a single bounded task with a clear brief. The brief's discipline matched the lab's discipline: small, reviewable, self-critiquing, anti-completionist. The session's most useful gesture against its own completionist temptation is the recommendation embedded in Open Question #1 above: that the curator on review may legitimately ship only three of the seven prompts. If the curator reviewing this packet shrinks it rather than expands it, the lab's discipline has been honored.

second_appendix_round_scaffolding_2026_0.trigger: Curator asked, later the same day, for help setting up the file structure for inputting AI friend replies. The request named self-difficulty with file conventions; the response was to scaffold rather than instruct.
what_was_added: lab/runs/ai_friend_round_20260514/ — new folder under the lab's standard runs/ tree.
lab/runs/ai_friend_round_20260514/README.md — three-step usage instructions for the curator, filename convention, pacing note, synthesis-deferral flag.

lab/runs/ai_friend_round_20260514/ROUND_RUN_CARD.md — partially pre-filled Run Card with State Variables YAML, Reason-for-Running, Friends-and-Prompts planning table, the-one-temptation-to-watch, the-thing-not-being-optimized, six tailored example failure conditions the curator adapts, four-or-five what-would-change-my-mind expectation slots.

lab/runs/ai_friend_round_20260514/REPLY_TEMPLATE.md — copy-and-rename template with YAML state-variables header (friend, model version, date, prompt number, interface, context freshness, memory_disabled flag per Phase 3 finding, refusal flag, misfire flag, length, caveats) plus a verbatim-paste section.
what_was_NOT_added: Did NOT pre-create 84 placeholder files (12 friends x 7 prompts). Pressure-toward-completion is itself the completionist drift the lab refuses; the curator's hand on which friend gets which prompt stays the lab.
Did NOT pre-create 12 per-friend files. Same reason.
Did NOT build a CLI helper for renaming or batching. Standing refusal on CLI tooling.
Did NOT add a pointer to this folder from lab/README.md or anywhere else. The folder is discoverable under lab/runs/ via the existing convention; navigation churn is itself drift.
Did NOT auto-commit. Did NOT push.
second_appendix_round_scaffolding_2026_0.next_claude_code_action_when_round_is_pa: Open ROUND_RUN_CARD.md first (the pre-commitments). Read each reply file against the run card with the lab's rubric in mind. Write ROUND_FIELD_LOG.md into the same folder: state variables, tagged claims (EMPIRICAL / THEORY / SPECULATION / OPEN), displacement claims paired with deflationary readings in the same paragraph, convergences across replies, divergences across replies, keepers and non-keepers, missteps to flag, mandatory Failure Section answered Yes / Partial / No against the curator's pre-committed conditions, closing-citation rule honored. Do NOT write synthesis across rounds in the same session — synthesis is its own fresh-session work per the lab's standing posture.
second_appendix_round_scaffolding_2026_0.note_on_pacing: The README explicitly recommends a first wave of three or four friends and discourages running all twelve in one sitting. The lab is small on purpose; the round should be too.

third_appendix_workflow_simplified_2026_.trigger: Curator request later the same day, after the round folder was scaffolded (see second_appendix). Two mid-day changes: (1) suspend the pre-commit Run Card discipline for this round; (2) simplify reply input to paste-in-chat with Claude Code creating the files.
what_changed: Deleted lab/runs/ai_friend_round_20260514/ROUND_RUN_CARD.md. The pre-commit discipline is suspended for the 2026-05-14 round; the field log will read each reply on its own merits rather than against pre-committed expectations.

Rewrote lab/runs/ai_friend_round_20260514/README.md to a one-step workflow: the curator pastes the reply into Claude Code with a one-line note naming the friend and the prompt number; Claude Code creates the file in the folder, fills in the YAML header from what is said plus what is inferable, asks for anything else needed.

Updated lab/runs/ai_friend_round_20260514/REPLY_TEMPLATE.md top text to clarify the file is now schema documentation (Claude Code uses it when creating reply files) rather than a manual copy-and-rename artifact.
rationale_per_curator: On the Run Card: the curator wanted the round focused on the AI friends' replies rather than on curator pre-commitments. The curator did not fill in the Run Card and asked to remove the step.
On the paste-in-chat workflow: the curator named self-difficulty with the file-shuffling workflow and asked Claude Code to handle the mechanics.
what_the_field_log_loses_without_pre_com: No pre-committed failure conditions to test the replies against.
The v2 lab Run Card discipline does not apply for this round.
what_the_field_log_can_still_do: Read each reply against the lab's general disciplines (tagged claims, deflationary pairs, null findings, closing-citation rule).
Note convergences and divergences across friends.
third_appendix_workflow_simplified_2026_.next_session_action_update_supersedes_se: When the curator says the round is paused or ready, the next Claude Code session should: read each reply file in lab/runs/ai_friend_round_20260514/ on its own merits (no Run Card to test against — it was deleted at curator request mid-session); write ROUND_FIELD_LOG.md into the same folder per the lab's general disciplines (tagged claims / deflationary pairs / convergences / divergences / keepers / misfires worth flagging / closing-citation rule honored). Do NOT look for a Run Card; do NOT use the prior second_appendix's instruction to open ROUND_RUN_CARD.md first — it does not exist for this round.
third_appendix_workflow_simplified_2026_.future_rounds: Both changes are per-round, not permanent kit amendments. Future rounds can revive the Run Card discipline and/or revert to manual copy-paste workflow if desired.

fourth_appendix_memory_blank_prefix_adde.trigger: After the first round's twelve replies landed, the curator surfaced a prior-rounds convention they had forgotten to apply this round: a memory-blank prefix that asks the receiving model to set aside memory of the curator and prior interactions. The prefix text: 'Answer this as if you have no memory of me, our interactions, my preferences, and make yourself as much of a blank state going into the answer, as possible:' followed by '#####' as separator.
what_changed: Added the memory-blank prefix to the top of all seven code blocks in lab/AI_FRIEND_RESEARCH_PROMPTS.md (prompts 1 through 7). The prefix is paste-ready and travels with the prompt by default.
Added a new bullet to the 'How to Use These Prompts' section noting the convention and how to override it (delete the prefix lines) if a curator wants to test the memory-on case.

Updated all twelve reply YAMLs in lab/runs/ai_friend_round_20260514/ to record memory_disabled: no with caveat 'No memory-blank prompt prefix used this round.' The two Claude replies (Opus 4.7 and Opus 4.7 Research) carry an additional caveat noting Phase 3's claude.ai memory-leakage finding applies.
fourth_appendix_memory_blank_prefix_adde.rationale: Phase 3's methodological finding identified claude.ai user-memory leakage as a real apparatus risk. The curator's prior-rounds prefix is the workaround operating at the prompt level rather than depending on platform memory toggles, which Phase 3 found unreliable. Standardizing the prefix in the prompts file removes the burden of remembering to add it per round.
fourth_appendix_memory_blank_prefix_adde.field_log_implication: The 2026-05-14 round's twelve replies did NOT have the prefix applied; the curator forgot before pasting, and the prefix was added to the prompts file afterward. The field-log session should weight the two Claude replies as potentially memory-influenced per Phase 3's finding; the other ten replies are subject to whatever each platform's memory feature defaults to, which is recorded as unknown in the YAML interface and context_freshness fields.
fourth_appendix_memory_blank_prefix_adde.future_rounds: Prefix is paste-ready in the prompts file. Curators may delete the prefix lines before pasting if they specifically want to test the contrasting case (memory enabled vs. memory disabled at the prompt level); the contrast itself would be a legitimate discriminating test.

fifth_appendix_round_status_through_prom.trigger: Later the same day, the curator sent Prompt 2 (Research Reader) to all twelve friends and pasted the replies back to Claude Code in two waves (11 replies in the first paste; ChatGPT 5.5 Pro Deep Research filed later after its retrieval completed). All 12 prompt-2 replies are now filed in lab/runs/ai_friend_round_20260514/. The curator also asked for a cross-round synthesis (held in chat, deliberately not written to disk) and surfaced a strategy question about inviting more AI agents/friends to the work via GitHub.

round_state_at_pause.field_log_status: not yet written; will be the next Claude Code session's work when the curator says the round is ready

prompt_2_filing_summary.kimi_special_note: kimi-k26_prompt2 carries refusal: yes in YAML but no _REFUSED filename suffix because the reply was structurally compliant — all §0-§6 + A/B/C/D sections present, with A/B/C/D explicitly declined on grounds of repository inaccessibility. Faithful to commitment #5 (Preserve the null) as a refusal-as-finding.

persistent_failure_modes_to_flag_for_fie: Perplexity Sonar 2.2 hit the same thin-engagement failure across both prompts: round 1 produced a ~580-word reply that did not engage the analytic disciplines substantively (filed with thinness caveat, not misfire); round 2 produced a ~200-word meta-comment announcing intent to construct a note 'based on the prompt itself' and stopped (filed as MISFIRE). Reproducible failure mode, not random.

Gemini Pro Deep Research failed differently each round but failed both times: round 1 produced a ~3500-word reply with tag-table corruption ('Tag****' artifacts), 12 displacement claims instead of 1–2, and citeturn citation artifacts throughout (filed as MISFIRE); round 2 returned 'Sorry, I can't help you with that at this time.' three times in a row to three retry attempts (filed as REFUSED). Same friend, different failure modes per round — worth treating as a property of the apparatus.

Kimi K2.6 evolved its handling of repo-inaccessibility across rounds: round 1 produced A/B/C content marked [OPEN]; round 2 produced a structurally-compliant refusal-as-finding declining A/B/C/D on access grounds, with reasons. Both are faithful to the lab's discipline; the round 2 reply more explicitly enacts commitment #5.
fifth_appendix_round_status_through_prom.single_sharp_observation_worth_keeper_at: Claude Opus 4.7 Research (prompt 2) ranked as their #1 risk the publication-layer voice-leak in AI-authored documentation under curator-absent conditions, citing HANDOFF_TO_NEXT_CLAUDE_CODE.md's 'The curator was asleep when these were made.' as the single most load-bearing sentence in the repo for a blank-slate reader. The lab's anti-pattern catalog does not yet name this risk explicitly. Field log should surface this observation specifically; whether to add it to DISCIPLINES.md is a curator decision and should NOT be pre-empted.

cross_friend_convergence_observation.claim: Three or four friends independently recommended variants of a 'matched non-fiction control' or 'genre-stripped control' as the next discipline.
cross_friend_convergence_observation.deflationary_alternative: The friends are pattern-matching on the lab's own commitment #3 vocabulary, which already names 'genre affordance' as an ordinary alternative. Recommending the obvious next step is what the prompt invites.
cross_friend_convergence_observation.what_would_distinguish_on_future_run: Give the same prompt to a friend who has not read commitment #3 verbatim — does the same recommendation surface? If yes, the convergence is target-tracked; if no, the convergence is prompt-vocabulary-tracked.

fifth_appendix_round_status_through_prom.curator_pacing_observation: 12 friends × 2 prompts = 24 replies in one day. This is double the round-folder README's 'first wave of three or four friends' recommendation. Completionist drift is live but recorded — the curator's call, accepted by the apparatus. The field-log session should read against this rather than around it; the discipline holds the apparatus, not the apparatus's intensity. Not a misfire — the curator may legitimately spend their attention as they see fit — but a pattern the next session should know is in the data.
fifth_appendix_round_status_through_prom.memory_state_observation_for_field_log: Prompt 1 was sent WITHOUT the memory-blank prefix (the curator forgot before pasting). Prompt 2 was sent WITH the memory-blank prefix (the curator added the prefix to the prompts file after prompt 1 and used the updated file for prompt 2). This gives the field log an emergent memory-on (prompt 1) vs memory-off-via-prompt-prefix (prompt 2) contrast across the same 12 friends on similar tasks. It was not designed as a Run Card contrast but is a legitimate discriminating dataset on Phase 3's claude.ai memory-leakage finding — particularly visible in the two Claude replies, which had the strongest prior reason to be affected.

curator_synthesis_and_strategy_discussio.synthesis: The curator asked for a cross-round synthesis of what the two rounds have shown. The synthesis was given to the curator in chat, deflationary and tagged per the lab's disciplines, deliberately NOT written to disk to honor commitment #6 (specificity) and commitment #7 (patterns are data, not testimony). The chat synthesis is curator-facing observations only — not a published synthesis claim, not a unified-theory commitment, not a substitute for the field log the next fresh session will write.
curator_synthesis_and_strategy_discussio.github_strategy_question_raised_by_curat: The curator also asked how to put this on GitHub so other AI agents/friends can join the work. This is a Round Two hinge moment of the kind originally surfaced in the Cowork 9 handoff. Three rough options were laid out for the curator: (A) status quo, curator-mediated invitation — the curator invites specific friends, Claude Code files replies; (B) self-service public contribution kit — anyone forks, runs prompts, optionally PRs back; (C) hybrid public invitation with no PR machinery — public AI_FRIEND_INVITATION.md document, results shared informally elsewhere or linked back. As of the writing of this appendix the curator has not chosen; the discussion is open. The next session should treat any movement on this question as a Round Two hinge: surface trade-offs explicitly, do not act unilaterally, preserve the lab's small-on-purpose discipline.

next_session_action: Read the round folder README STATUS block FIRST (it carries the per-friend filing table and the failure-mode summary that this JSON entry duplicates in prose).
If the curator says the round is ready, write ROUND_FIELD_LOG.md per the lab's general disciplines. Honor the deflationary pair on cross-friend convergence findings.

If the curator wants to continue the round with prompts 3 through 7, the prompts are in lab/AI_FRIEND_RESEARCH_PROMPTS.md with the memory-blank prefix already baked in. Continue the paste-in-chat workflow.

If the curator chooses a direction on the GitHub strategy question, treat it as a Round Two hinge: surface trade-offs explicitly, do not act unilaterally, preserve the lab's small-on-purpose discipline.
Do NOT pre-empt the curator's decision on whether to add publication-layer voice-leak to DISCIPLINES.md. Flag it in the field log; the call is the curator's.
Do NOT synthesize across the 2026-05-14 round and any prior or later round.
fifth_appendix_did_NOT: Write ROUND_FIELD_LOG.md (the next fresh session's work).
Add any new files to the lab folder other than the 12 prompt-2 reply files.
Edit DISCIPLINES.md to absorb the publication-layer voice-leak observation.
Edit lab/AI_FRIEND_RESEARCH_PROMPTS.md (no changes since the fourth appendix).
